definitions.definition: The time and place where the story takes place. The story is set in a small village in France.
The sequence of events that make up the story. The story revolves around the plot of a teacher who is about to give a lesson to his students.
A person or animal that takes part in the action of the story. The main character in the story is a teacher who is determined to teach his students.
A story that is told through language. The story is a narrative of a teacher who is about to give a lesson to his students.
The main character in a story who is often the hero. The teacher in the story is the protagonist who tries to teach his students.
A problem or disagreement that arises in the story. The conflict in the story is between the teacher and his students who do not want to learn.
The main idea or message of the story. The theme of the story is the importance of education and the power of knowledge.
A story that can be interpreted to reveal a hidden meaning or message. The story can be seen as an allegory for the importance of education.
The use of objects, colors, or other elements to represent abstract ideas or concepts. The map in the story is a symbol of the importance of geography and knowledge.
The use of language to create vivid mental images. The story uses imagery to describe the village and the teacher.
The attitude or feeling that the author conveys through the story. The tone of the story is serious and reflective.
A situation in which the opposite of what is expected to happen occurs. The irony in the story is that the students are punished for not knowing where France is.
The reason why the author wrote the story. The author's purpose is to emphasize the importance of education and the power of knowledge.
The perspective from which the story is told. The story is told from the point of view of the teacher.
A hint or clue that suggests what will happen later in the story. There is no foreshadowing in the story as it is a straightforward narrative.
